description: In a brightly lit apartment kitchen, Ross and Rachel stand facing each other. Ross says, "Be like a private, personal thing between us, okay?" Rachel raises both hands to her chest and replies, "Ross, Phoebe is my girlfriend, okay? We tell each other everything. You know?" She then gestures outward.
Rachel turns slightly away, waving her hand, and says, "I mean, come on. Guys do the same thing. I mean, what about that locker room stuff?"
Ross, holding a wrapped ice cream bar, replies, "That's different, okay? That's like," pointing toward her with his free hand.
Ross continues, "who dated a stripper? Or who did it on the back of the Staten Island Ferry?" He gestures with the ice cream bar while Rachel stands with her arms crossed.
An off-screen male voice asks, "Both of those, Joey?" Another off-screen male voice replies, "Yeah."
Ross looks toward the off-screen speakers.

Ross turns back to Rachel. He points the ice cream bar at her and says, "but you don't, you don't talk about, like, your girlfriend and the intimate stuff you do with her." Rachel maintains her crossed arms.
Rachel keeps her arms crossed, maintaining her posture.
Rachel asks, "Not even with your best friend?" Ross shakes his head slightly and says, "No." Rachel replies, "That is so sad"
Rachel continues looking at Ross with her arms crossed. Ross holds his gaze on her.
Rachel unfolds her arms and gestures with both hands as she says, "you're missing out on so much, Ross. I mean, the bonding and the know and knowing that"
Rachel points toward herself, then extends her hand toward Ross, saying, "someone else is going through the same thing you are." Ross asks, "So what, you"
Ross looks at Rachel, who stands with her arms at her sides.
Ross finishes his question, "tell each other everything?"
Rachel presses her lips together and looks at Ross.
Rachel tilts her head and replies, "Pretty much."
Ross looks at her, then his gaze shifts briefly downward. Rachel stands with her arms at her sides.
Ross raises his eyebrows and asks, "Did you talk about the night of five times?" Rachel raises a hand to her neck, her eyes widening.
Rachel crosses her arms again and looks upward, pressing her lips together.
Ross smiles slightly, pointing the ice cream bar at her, and asks, "Do you tell people about the night of five times?"
Rachel keeps her arms crossed and lowers her gaze.
Rachel says, "Honey? Yeah, that was with Carol." Ross takes a bite of the ice cream and replies, "I know, but it's still worth mentioning, I think."
The setting changes to a dimly lit bedroom illuminated by candles. Monica lies in bed under the covers while Phoebe sits beside her.
Phoebe gestures slowly with one hand and says in a soft volume, "Relax every muscle in your body."
Phoebe picks up a small bottle and opens it. Monica rests her head on the pillow.
Faint instrumental music starts playing. Phoebe adds drops from the bottle into a lit candle and says in a soft volume, "Listen to the plinky plunky music."
Phoebe sets the bottle down and gently touches Monica's covered arm.
Phoebe waves her hand slowly over Monica's face. "Okay, now close your eyes and think of a happy," she says in a soft volume. Monica closes her eyes.
Phoebe lowers her hand and sits back slightly.
Phoebe asks, "Okay, tell me your happy place." Monica, eyes still closed, replies, "Richard's. Living room. Drinking?"
Phoebe shakes her head quickly.
Phoebe says, "No, no, not a Richard thing." She gestures with both hands. "Just put down the glass and get out."
Monica shifts in bed and opens her eyes.
Monica says, "Sorry, but that's my happy place." Phoebe replies, "Well, okay, fine. Use my happy," gesturing toward herself.
Phoebe holds her hands up, palms out, and says, "I'm just I'm going to have to ask that you don't move anything there."
Monica looks at Phoebe.
Monica says, "All right, I'll try not to." Phoebe brings her hands together and begins, "All right, so you're in a meadow,"
Phoebe gestures broadly with both hands.
Phoebe continues, "millions of stars in the sky," wiggling her fingers as she raises her hands. Monica opens her eyes and asks, "You think breaking up with him was a huge mistake?"
Phoebe points a finger and says, "Okay, there are no questions in the happy place,"
Monica turns her head away slightly.
Phoebe continues, "okay? Just the warm breeze and the moonlight flowing through the trees," moving her hands slowly.
Monica interrupts, "I'll bet he's totally over me. I'll bet"
Monica finishes, "he's fine." Phoebe raises both hands in a stopping motion and says, "Betting and wagering of any kind are, I'm sure."
Phoebe finishes, "Not permitted in the happy place. Okay?"
Monica turns her head on the pillow. Phoebe says, "There's just the lovely waterfalls and the trickling fountains and the"
Phoebe makes slow waving motions with her hands and says, "calming sounds of the babbling brook."
Monica sits up slightly, propping herself on her elbows.
Monica says, "Okay, this isn't working." She shifts further up in the bed. "I'm still awake, and now I have to pee."
Monica pushes herself up and begins to move the covers.
In an apartment kitchen, Joey stands near the counter and a foosball table. He steps toward the apartment door.
Joey points toward the door and mimics a nasal voice, saying, "Guess who?" Audience laughter is audible.
Joey lowers his hand, turns away from the door, then turns back, stepping closer to it.
Joey opens the door. Janice stands in the hallway wearing a bright green suit, her arms crossed.
Joey says, "Janice, hi. You just missed Chandler. But won't you come in before you go?" Janice steps into the apartment and stops, keeping her arms crossed, as audience laughter sounds.
Joey closes the door behind her and turns back, standing near the foosball table.
Janice tilts her head and says, "So I hear you hate me."
Joey raises his eyebrows, his mouth opening slightly as he looks at her.

Joey points his finger and says, "I never said hate. I was very careful about that." Janice replies, gesturing with her hands, "A little birdie told me something about you wanting to rip your arm off"
Janice continues her hand gestures, adding, "and throw it at me." Audience laughter is heard.
Joey holds out his hand, palm up.
Joey says, "You got hate from"
Joey gestures with his hand and lowers his head briefly.
Joey raises his hand again, finishing, "taking a big leap."
Joey walks past Janice toward the living area.
Janice says, "Fine," her hands raised.
Janice points her finger and says, "Fine. We have got to do something about our little situation here."
Janice lowers her hands, then raises them open-palmed, saying, "Joey. So this is my idea. You and me spending some quality time together."
Joey stands with his hands in his pockets, his brow furrowed.
Joey asks, gesturing with one hand, "But what does that" before Janice interrupts, "for Chandler." Audience laughter sounds.
Joey lowers his head and nods slightly.

Joey points a finger and says, "Okay. I'm in." Janice leans forward, extending her arms, and says, "Okay. All right. This is what we're going to call it. Joey and Janice's Day of Fun." Laughter is audible.
Janice grabs Joey's arm and pulls him toward the door.
Joey resists her pull, asking, "Does it have to be a whole day?" Janice stands close to him, replying, "Yes, because that's how long it takes to love me." Laughter is heard.
Joey looks down, his expression unchanging.
Joey says, "I know."
Joey turns away, adding, "I sleep in the next room," as he walks toward his bedroom, followed by laughter.
The setting changes to an exterior view of a city skyline at dusk. Inside a coffee shop, Chandler stands holding a mug next to Rachel, while Ross and Phoebe sit on an adjacent sofa.
Chandler looks toward Ross and Phoebe, asking, "So do you guys all hate Janice too?"
Ross lowers his newspaper and asks, "Oh, you hate Janice now?"
Phoebe, sitting next to Ross, smiles and says, "Oh, Joey's going to be so relieved." Audience laughter sounds.
Chandler looks at Phoebe, his brow furrowed, while Rachel touches her chin.
Monica approaches the window from outside, holding a cardboard box, crying. Rachel turns and says, "Oh, my God," as laughter is heard.
Monica presses her face against the window glass, sobbing visibly. Chandler turns his head to look at her.
Chandler looks back toward Ross and Phoebe, saying, "Well, look, everybody. It's Weepy,"
Chandler watches the window.
Chandler finishes, "the mime that cares too much," followed by audience laughter.
Monica enters the coffee shop, holding the box, still sobbing. Rachel walks toward her.
Monica stops near the sofa, crying as she speaks. "Then I went down to the post office. It turns out it was those videos that I ordered for Richard about the Civil War."
Ross looks at Monica, holding his newspaper.
Monica continues to sob, taking VHS tapes out of the box. She cries out, "We love the Civil War." Audience laughter sounds.
Phoebe walks over to Monica, placing a hand on her arm.
Phoebe asks gently, "Monica, do you want us to take you home?"
Monica nods, sobbing, and turns toward the door. Rachel guides her out.
Phoebe gestures vaguely and adds, "Or maybe to a galaxy far, far away," as Monica exits. Laughter is heard.
The women exit the coffee shop. Ross watches them leave, turning his head.
Chandler sits down in the armchair adjacent to the sofa, crossing one leg over the other. Ross adjusts his position on the sofa.
Chandler leans toward Ross and asks, "Women tell each other everything. Did you know that?" Ross nods and replies, "Yeah."
Chandler keeps his gaze on Ross.
Ross leans in slightly, saying, "No, Chandler. Everything."
Ross continues, "Like"
Ross pauses.
Ross says, "stuff you like."
Ross adds, "Stuff she likes."
Ross says, "Technique."
Ross lists, "Stamina, girth," gesturing with his hand.
Chandler's eyes widen.
Chandler asks, "Girth?" Laughter sounds.
Chandler leans further back in his chair.
Chandler asks, his mouth open, "Why"
Chandler holds his expression.
Chandler completes his sentence, "would they do this?" Laughter is audible.
Ross leans his head back against the sofa.
Ross says, "Rachel says sharing's great and"
Ross gestures with his hands.
Ross finishes, "know we ought to be doing it."
Chandler looks at Ross, motionless.
Ross gestures toward Chandler, asking, "Do you wanna?"
Chandler keeps his gaze forward, then slowly turns his head to look at Ross.
Chandler shakes his head slightly, asking, "We're not going to talk about girth, are we?" Laughter is heard.
Ross shakes his head.
Chandler nods and says, "Yeah."
Chandler says, "Okay. Yeah." Ross gestures toward Chandler, saying, "All right, you go." Chandler replies, "Okay."
Ross raises a hand and says, "Okay, I'll go first."
Ross leans closer, gesturing with one hand as he says, "Okay. So the other night, Rachel and I are in bed talking about fantasies and I happened"
Ross lowers his voice slightly, continuing, "to describe a particular"
Ross pauses, moving his hand.
Ross adds, "Star Wars thing." Chandler quickly interjects, "Princess Leia in the gold bikini?" Ross nods and says, "Yes," as the audience cheers and laughs.
Chandler grins broadly, shaking his head. Ross looks back at him.
In a coffee shop, Chandler and Ross sit together on a couch. Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Well, wow. Well, that was easy." Ross listens attentively.
Chandler points a thumb toward Ross and says, "Okay, you go."
Ross nods slightly and says, "Okay."
Chandler taps his own chest and raises his hands.
Chandler points a finger toward Ross and says, "Well, you know when you're in bed with a woman and"
Chandler continues gesturing with his hands.
Chandler says, "you're fooling around with her and you get all these mental images in your brain? Like Elle Macpherson or that girl at the" while gesturing toward his head.
Ross gestures near his stomach and says, "Xerox place with the belly button," overlapping with Chandler.
Chandler nods and waves a hand.
Chandler says, "know? And then all of a sudden, your mom pops into your head," pointing at Ross and then touching his own head.
Chandler continues looking at Ross.
Chandler opens his arms wide, his jaw dropped, and says, "and you're like, mom, get out of here." Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler raises his hands as audience laughter continues.
Chandler gestures rapidly, saying, "But of course, after that, you can't possibly think of anything else and you can't stop what you're doing. So it's kind of like you're"
Chandler stammers silently, making erratic hand gestures. Audience laughter continues.
Chandler lowers his hands and says, "you don't know." Ross stares at him silently.
Ross maintains a steady gaze on Chandler. Chandler looks back, pressing his lips together. The audience laughs.
Ross leans slightly forward, raising his volume as he asks, "You're telling me about your mom? What is the matter with you?"
Ross maintains a hard stare at Chandler.
Ross points sharply toward another area of the coffee shop, saying, "I said share, not scare. Go sit over there." Chandler stands up and walks away.
The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night.
Inside an apartment, Chandler stands in the kitchen area. The front door opens, and Joey and Janice walk in. Janice carries a large blue stuffed bear and a small pennant flag.
Chandler turns toward them and asks, "Hey, what are you guys doing together?"
Janice and Joey smile as they step fully inside.
Janice wraps an arm around Joey and says loudly, "Joey and Janice's Day of Fun," waving the pennant. Joey grins.
Audience laughter is audible as Chandler watches them.
Chandler asks, "Really?" Joey holds up a takeout container and replies, "Yeah. Yeah, we went to a Mets game. We got Chinese food."
Joey lowers the container.
Joey gestures toward Janice with his free hand and adds, "And I love this woman."
Chandler stands still, watching them as audience laughter is heard.
Janice points the pennant handle at Chandler and says, "You have got competition, buddy."
Janice walks past the kitchen counter toward Chandler.
Janice says, "I just came by to give you a kiss," as she approaches him.
Janice leans in toward Chandler.
Janice kisses Chandler on the cheek, steps back, and says, "I have to go pick up the baby, so I'll see you later, sweetheart."
Walking toward the door, Janice looks at Joey and adds, "You too, Chandler."
Janice exits. Joey closes the door behind her and leans his back against it.
Joey drops his smile and exhales deeply, his shoulders slumping. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "You still can't stand it, can you?" Joey walks forward, gesturing, and replies, "I'm sorry, man. I tried. I really"
Joey continues walking into the kitchen area, tossing the takeout box onto the counter.
Chandler gestures with one hand and says, "well, you know, I appreciate you giving it a shot."
Joey stops and gestures with both hands, saying, "But, hey, look, you know, the good thing is that we spent the whole day together."
Joey points to his own chest and says, "And I survived."
Joey points a finger and says, "And what's even more amazing, so did she. It was Bat Day at Shea Stadium."
Chandler looks at Joey as audience laughter is heard.
Chandler nods slightly and says, "I guess that's something."
Joey gestures emphatically and says, "Oh, man, that's huge. Now I know I can stand to be around her. Which means I get to hang out with you."
Joey lowers his hands.
Joey says, "Which is kind of the whole point anyway."
Chandler gazes steadily at Joey.
Chandler says, "Okay."
Chandler and Joey step forward and embrace briefly. An audience "aww" is audible. Joey turns to walk away.
Joey points back toward Chandler and says, "Oh, hey, Chandler."
Joey stops, gesturing vaguely with one hand, and says, "We stopped by the coffee shop and ran into Ross."
Chandler's smile fades, and he looks downward slightly. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler looks back at Joey and says quietly, "Oh, God."
Joey watches Chandler.
Joey gestures with both hands and says, "If it makes you feel any better, I do it too."
Chandler stares at Joey silently.
Chandler asks, "Really?"
Joey nods and replies, "Oh, yeah."
Joey points a finger at Chandler and says, "I always picture your mom when I'm having sex."
Joey nods firmly and turns to walk away. Chandler stares after him with an open mouth and wide eyes. Audience laughter is heard.

In an apartment living room, Monica lies on a white couch under a plaid blanket. She holds a lit cigar and takes a drag, exhaling smoke. A male voice from an off-screen television says, "April 12, 1861."
Monica continues resting on the couch, holding the cigar near her mouth. The television narrator continues speaking.
The narrator says, "United States Garrison in Fort Sumter was fired upon." Monica holds the cigar, staring straight ahead.
Monica points a remote control forward. The television narrator says, "It is now under bombardment," and the audio stops. Monica sits up slightly.
Monica sets the remote down, places the cigar in an ashtray on the coffee table, and stands up, leaving the blanket on the couch.
Wearing an oversized button-down shirt and pajama pants, she walks toward the apartment door.
A knock sounds at the door. Monica opens it, revealing her father in the hallway.
Monica asks, "Hey, dad. When you doing here?"
Her father enters, spreading his arms, and says, "Well, it's your mother's bridge night so I thought I'd come into the city for a little Monica cuddle."
The father wraps his arms around Monica in a hug, then steps back and looks at her.
The father gestures with one hand toward the living room and asks, "Since when did you start smoking cigars?"
Monica, now holding the cigar in her right hand, replies, "I don't."
Monica turns and walks toward the kitchen area, raising the cigar slightly as she says, "I just like the smell of them."
Monica continues walking past the couch.
Monica turns to face him, asking, "So, what are you really doing here, dad?" Her father stands near the door and replies, "Well, I just wanted to make sure that you're okay."
Monica tucks a strand of hair behind her ear, maintaining eye contact.
Monica asks, "What makes you think that I might not be okay?" Her father replies, "I saw Richard."
Monica's eyes widen slightly.
Monica says, "Oh."
The father approaches her, asking, "So how you doing?"
Monica walks to the couch and sits down, saying, "I'm fine. Just a little tired, but I'm okay."
Monica reaches for the plaid blanket on the couch.
Monica continues adjusting the blanket, asking, "How's Richard doing?"
The father, standing nearby with his hands in his pockets, says, "You don't want to know."
The father stands with his hands in his pockets while Monica looks up at him.
Monica sits up straighter on the couch, looking at him, and says, "No, I really, really do."
The father starts to sit down on the coffee table.
The father sits on the coffee table facing her. He rests his hands on his knees and says, "Well, he's doing terrible."
Monica stares at him, her lips parted slightly.
Monica asks, "Really?"
The father nods and smiles softly.
The father says, "Worse than when he broke up with Barbara?"
Monica raises her hand toward his arm.
Monica touches his arm, leaning in to ask, "You're not just saying that, are you?" He shakes his head, replying, "No. The man is a mess."
Monica covers her mouth with her hand briefly, her gaze steady on him.
Monica asks, "Was he crying?" The father shakes his head, replying, "No."
Monica shifts her gaze slightly, keeping her hand near her face.
Monica leans forward slightly and asks, "Well, do you think he was waiting till after you left so he could cry?"
The father nods and says, "Maybe."
Monica smiles and tilts her head back briefly.
Monica raises her arms behind her head, leaning back against the couch cushions, and says, "I think so." The father leans toward her, saying, "Honey, relationships are hard."
The father gestures with his hands, continuing, "Like with your mom and me. You know, after we graduated from college, we broke up for a while."
He clasps his hands, adding, "It seems her father, your..."
He continues, "...grandfather, wanted her to travel around Europe like he did." Monica rests her head back, her eyes closing.
He adds, "Of course, he got to do it on Uncle Sam's nickel because he was also..."
He finishes, "...strafing German troop trains at the time. However..." Audience laughter is heard as Monica remains motionless with her eyes closed.
The father looks at Monica, noticing she is asleep. He stops speaking and rests his hands on his legs.
He stands up from the coffee table and leans over Monica.
He grasps the plaid blanket and pulls it up to cover her shoulders.
He leans down, kisses her on the forehead, and steps back from the couch.
He sits back down on the coffee table, picks up the remote control, and looks toward the television.
He picks up the cigar, places it in his mouth, takes a puff, and presses a button on the remote.
The screen turns completely black.
The setting changes to a bedroom. A dark-haired man lies in bed under a striped blanket, propped against pillows. He hums a tune.
A woman enters through a doorway, wearing a white patterned bathrobe over a gold metal bikini top. The man turns his head toward her and smiles.
The woman removes her robe, revealing the metal bikini top and a pink skirt. Her hair is styled in two large buns. She steps toward the bed.
The woman stands near the bed, holding her removed robe. She says, "Okay, here we go."
The woman says, "I am Jabba's prisoner, and you have a really weird look on your face." The man stares at her, his mouth slightly open.
The man continues staring at her with his mouth slightly open.
The woman touches her hair buns and asks, "What is it? Did I get it wrong? Did I get the hair wrong?"
The woman gestures with her hand and asks, "Did you just picture it?"
The man looks away from her, shaking his head slightly, and replies, "No, it's not you. It's..."
The man continues shaking his head, his gaze directed forward, away from the woman.
In a bedroom doorway, an older woman with her hair styled in two large side buns stands facing forward. She wears a gold neck ring and a light floral robe. Audience laughter is audible.
The older woman says, "well, what is it?"
Moving her head slightly, the older woman continues, "Come on, sweetie. Are you kind of, like, freaking me out here?" The audience laughter grows louder.
Ross sits in bed, leaning against the pillows and wearing a grey t-shirt. He stares forward, his mouth slightly open, and exhales audibly. The audience laughter continues.
Ross blinks and says, "I hate Chandler. The bastard ruined my life."

In the doorway, Rachel stands wearing the same hair buns, gold neck ring, and floral robe, which is open to reveal a gold bikini top. She looks toward Ross, lowers her gaze, and turns away, pulling the robe closed with her right hand as she walks out. Audience laughter swells, and upbeat instrumental music begins.
A block of white text over a dark red background fills the screen. The upbeat music continues.
The screen turns black. The music continues playing.
The screen remains black as the music fades out, leaving silence.
